page: Microsoft Azure E64-32ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Edsv6 server family. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, this server features 16 physical cores and 32 dedicated vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per vCPU. Storage capabilities are supported by 3780 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and the instance is premium storage capable. The virtualization layer is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in memory-intensive and single-threaded operations. The server achieves a strong read-only large-block memory bandwidth of 181.85 GB/sec, though its Passmark memory score falls into the lower quartile. Computational performance is highlighted by a strong Geekbench single-core score of 2110.0 and a multi-core score of 16603.0. Single-threaded gzip compression is highly efficient at 31.08 GB/sec, and static web serving throughput reaches a strong 43.34 GB/sec. For language model workloads, the server handles text generation for a small 135M model at a strong 296.69 tokens/sec, but performance drops to a poor 2.45 tokens/sec for a large 70B model, illustrating the limitations of CPU-only execution on massive models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E64-32ds_v6 offers high resource density by bundling a massive 512 GB memory footprint with 3780 GB of local storage on a 32-vCPU profile. This configuration avoids the need to provision larger, more expensive compute instances solely to obtain sufficient memory or local disk capacity. Consequently, this server is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caching layers, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring high-speed local scratch space.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv6 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 physical cores and 32 dedicated vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of RAM and 3780 GB of local storage, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio. Benchmark results show strong performance in large-block memory read bandwidth (181.85 GB/sec), single-threaded gzip compression (31.08 GB/sec), and static web serving throughput (43.34 GB/sec). However, Passmark memory scores are relatively weak, and large-scale LLM inference is limited. Qualitatively, the server provides excellent resource density for memory-bound workloads without requiring excessive compute scaling. It is best suited for large databases, in-memory data stores, and data-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ds_v6 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering strong memory bandwidth and web serving performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ds_v6 is a memory-optimized server with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server featuring massive RAM and local storage for demanding database and data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs at 4.2 GHz
512 GB of system memory providing 16 GB of RAM per vCPU
3780 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong large-block memory read bandwidth of 181.85 GB/sec
Best suited for relational databases, in-memory caches, and data analytics
